title: pastinaca sativa subsp. urens
intro: Pastinaca sativa subsp. urens, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of wild parsnip include its potential to treat conditions such as gout, arthritis, and skin irritations, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of wild parsnip can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
